title: Support std::randomize in derived classes
body:
Consider the following (heavily minimised) test example:
```systemverilog
package my_pkg;
  import uvm_pkg::*;

  class foo_t extends uvm_sequence #(uvm_sequence_item);
    task my_task();
      int unsigned my_var;
      if (std::randomize(my_var) == 0) $fatal("Oh dear");
      $display("I randomized my_var and it is now %0d", my_var);
    endtask
  endclass
endpackage

module tb;
  initial begin
    automatic my_pkg::foo_t foo = new;
    foo.my_task();
  end
endmodule
```

I have the path to a copy of [chipsalliance/uvm-verilator](https://github.com/chipsalliance/uvm-verilator) in an environment variable called `UVM_CORE_PATH`, and building with the current HEAD of master (dc33e8bb1) gives the following:
```
$ ~/work/not-mine/verilator/bin/verilator --build-jobs 16 -o tb --main --binary --vpi --timing -I${UVM_CORE_PATH}    ${UVM_CORE_PATH}/dpi/uvm_dpi.cc    ${UVM_CORE_PATH}/uvm_pkg.sv rand.sv
%Error: rand.sv:8:26: 'randomize()' argument must be a variable contained in 'foo_t'
                    : ... note: In instance 'tb'
    8 |       if (std::randomize(my_var) == 0) $fatal("Oh dear");
      |                          ^~~~~~
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.047 for more assistance.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)

```

This seems to be something caused by a particular property of the code in `uvm_sequence`. When I tweak the example to derive from `uvm_sequence_base` instead (switching the line to `class foo_t extends uvm_sequence_base;`), the build runs happily, and I see:
```
$ ./obj_dir/tb 
UVM_INFO /nix/store/mmi19xi26q7f17z2m3z62dmrnm7m4xiz-uvm-core-snapshot/sv/base/uvm_root.svh(476) @ 0: reporter [UVM/RELNOTES] 
  ***********       IMPORTANT RELEASE NOTES         ************

  This implementation of the UVM Library deviates from the 1800.2-2020
  standard.  See the DEVIATIONS.md file contained in the release
  for more details.

----------------------------------------------------------------
Accellera:1800.2:UVM:2020.3.1

All copyright owners for this kit are listed in NOTICE.txt
All Rights Reserved Worldwide
----------------------------------------------------------------

      (Specify +UVM_NO_RELNOTES to turn off this notice)

I randomized my_var and it is now 2871082281
- S i m u l a t i o n   R e p o r t: Verilator 5.047 devel
- Verilator: end at 0s; walltime 0.002 s; speed 0.000 s/s
- Verilator: cpu 0.002 s on 1 threads; allocated 21 MB

```
code_blocks:

  import uvm_pkg::*;

  class foo_t extends uvm_sequence #(uvm_sequence_item);

endpackage

    foo.my_task();

endmodule

%Error: rand.sv:8:26: 'randomize()' argument must be a variable contained in 'foo_t'

      |                          ^~~~~~
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.047 for more assistance.
